description: Create a reusable TimezoneSelect component and replace the free-text timezone input on the registration page with a searchable dropdown of valid IANA timezones. Use the built-in Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone') API to generate the timezone list on the client side, with the user's detected timezone pre-selected via Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone.
steps: Create a new client component at app/components/TimezoneSelect.tsx that: (a) uses Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone') to get all valid IANA timezone strings, (b) renders a <select> dropdown with all timezones as options, (c) groups or formats the options for readability (e.g., showing 'America/New_York' as-is or with slash replaced by ' - '), (d) accepts name, id, and defaultValue props so it integrates with the registration form, (e) defaults the selected value to the user's browser-detected timezone using Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone, (f) uses the same Tailwind CSS styling pattern as other inputs in the registration form (see the input classes in app/register/page.tsx), (g) uses semantic HTML with proper label association, (h) includes responsive design with Tailwind classes

Update app/register/page.tsx to: (a) import and use the new TimezoneSelect component in place of the existing <input id='timezone' name='timezone' type='text' ...> element, (b) keep the same form field name 'timezone' so the server action in lib/auth-actions.ts continues to work without changes, (c) keep the same label text and styling

Verify that the server-side validation in lib/auth-actions.ts still works correctly since all values from Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone') are valid IANA timezone strings that will pass the existing Intl.DateTimeFormat validation
acceptance_criteria: The registration page shows a dropdown/select element for timezone instead of a text input
The dropdown contains all IANA timezone strings from Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')
The user's browser-detected timezone is pre-selected by default
The form submission still works - the 'timezone' field value is sent to the server action
The select element uses the same visual styling (Tailwind classes) as other form inputs
npm run build passes without errors
npm run lint passes without errors
verification: Run npm run build and confirm it exits with code 0
Run npm run lint and confirm it exits with code 0
findings: ESLint react-hooks/set-state-in-effect rule prevents calling setState inside useEffect. Refactored to use uncontrolled select with defaultValue computed synchronously via getDefaultTimezone helper function (no useState/useEffect needed). Review feedback addressed: moved Intl.supportedValuesOf call to module scope to avoid recomputation on every render, and grouped timezones by continent/region using optgroup elements for better UX. Build and lint pass cleanly.
